The '''''Hearst''''' was a Corellian [[Class VI bulk freighter]], launched in around [[70 BBY/Legends|70 BBY]] and used during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] as the mobile base of [[Freedom Galactic News]].

After a long career as a cargo hauler, the ''Hearst'' was modified at around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] to serve as the roving base of an independent newsfeed, broadcasting from the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]—and in this new role, the old [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] battered, time-worn [[Hull/Legends|hull]] concealed a bright, modern interior.

Although the aging drives did not provide enough power to allow the ship to operate its transmission array while in transit, the recording studio boasted top-of-the range production equipment and the main [[Computer/Legends|computer]] contained a powerful news database. The main [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] was a treasure-trove of useful scrap and junk, and the accommodation for the crew and news team was well-appointed, with a shared kitchen and living-room, and ten passenger cabins, each with ''en suite'' bathrooms.

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Hearst'' appears only in an issue of the French RPG magazine [[Casus Belli (magazine)|''Casus Belli'']], although another Class VI freighter would be shown a few months later in [[Heir to the Empire 3|''Heir to the Empire'' 3]].
